rulings.Case Title: PAUL EDGAR GORDON JR et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 05, 2008 LINE 18. DEFENDANT CHRYSLER, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WORKED ON ORIGINAL CHRYSLER BRAKES IN BOTH DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. THIS EVIDENCE HAS NOT BEEN NEGATED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION (FALSE REPRESENTATION), FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION (INTENTIONAL TORT) AND EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION (STRICT LIABILITY RELATED TO CLUTCHES AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES). PLAINTIF FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE ON THESE CAUSES OF ACTION THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THIRD, FOURTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO FIRST, SECOND, FIFTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON EXPOSURE TO NEW BRAKES. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: ENVIRONMENTAL WORLD WATCH,INC.,IN THE PUBLIC VS. AMERICAN AIRLINES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Air Canada, American Airlines, Inc., America West Airlines, Inc., Air Wisconsin Airlines Corporation, Alaska Airlines, Inc. Motion To Consolidate Actions Of Case(S) Cgc-05-447903, Cgc-06-452413, Cgc-06-455658, Cgc-07-462756 With Case Cgc-05-439749
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 05, 2008 LINE 19. DEFENDANTS AIR CANADA, AMERICAN AIRLINES, INC., AMERICA WEST AIRLINES, INC., AIR WISCONSIN AIRLINES CORP. AND ALASKA AIRLINES, INC.'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASES 447903, 452413, 455658 AND 462756 WITH CASE 439749 IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH WEHR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 05, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY. IT ALSO APPEARS THAT THERE IS NO BASIS FOR ADMITTING DECEDENT'S TESTIMONY AGAINST THIS DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO EXPLAIN THE DELAY BETWEEN FEBRUARY 7, 2008 AND NOW IN SEEKING TO COMPEL THE REQUESTED DEPOSITION APART FROM WRITING TWO LETTERS DATED JANUARY 30, 2008 AND APRIL 25, 2008. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION FAILS TO DELINEATE THE FACTS THAT CAN BE OBTAINED. ALL PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION DEMONSTRATES IS THAT IT IS POSSIBLE THAT DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT WAS ON A SHIP WHEN DECEDENT WAS PRESENT. THAT IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH EXPOSURE FROM A PRODUCT MANUFACTURED BY DEFENDANT. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JEAN PHLEGER VS. JOHN LEE HUDSON et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 05, 2008 LINE 4. CROSS-DEFENDANTS JOHN HUDSON AND SHANA HUDSON'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. PARAGRAPHS TWENTY AND THIRTY-ONE PROVIDE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 05, 2008 LINE 5. PLAINTIFF KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AS DIRECTED AGAINST SEBASTINELLI'S CLAIM BASED ON BEING A SHAREHOLDER. THE MOTION IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. KEARNEY ACKNOWLEDGES THAT A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION MAY PLACE STOCK IN TRUST AND ABSENT THE TRUST AGREEMENT DEFENDANT CANNOT SHOW THIS HAS FAILED TO OCCUR. THE KALLEN DECLARATION ALSO CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THIS ISSUE. THIS MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE OTHER CLAIMS. BANKRUPTCY IS A CONDITION OF ENFORCING SECTION 3.7 AND THAT HAS NOT BEEN SHOWN TO HAVE OCCURRED. SECTION 3.5 IS VAGUE AND AMBIGUOUS AND ON ITS FACE, CANNOT BE CONSTRUED TO BAR SEBASTINELLI'S CLAMINS AGAINST KEARNEY. THE PROVISION APPEARS TO RELATE CLAIMS BY THE BANK AGAINST THE BORROWER AND HOW THE BANK DISCHARGES THE DEBT. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA STURLA et al VS. W. B. COYLE et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 05, 2008 LINE 8. CROSS-DEFENDANT W. COYLE'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FIVE DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING A BASIS FOR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES 5, 9 AND 10 - 14. OVERRULED AS TO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES 1 - 4, 6 AND 15 - 22. SUSTAIN WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE EIGHT. LACHES IS NOT A DEFENSE TO AN ACTION AT LAW. =(302/PJM/X-MW)

rulings.Case Title: STUDIO MEHLER, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. QUAKERMAID CABINETS, INC., A PENNSYLVANIA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 05, 2008 LINE 9. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S DORIS MEHLER, STUDIO MEHLER, JAMES SAYLES AND KEN BUGHARDT'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF EXHIBITS JN1 - JN6. THE PUBLIC RECORD ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF IS A CORPORATION. DEFENDANT IS GRANTED TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE CROSS-COMPLAINT TO PLEAD FACTS THAT ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR ITS THEORY OF ESTOPPEL AND THAT SAYLES SIGNED THE CONTRACT IN HIS PERSONAL CAPACITY AS GUARANTOR. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA BORY et al VS. EMILIO MONTALVO et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 05, 2008 LINE 11. DEFENDANT EMILIO MONTALVO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES WHICH PLEAD SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT CLAIMS FOR WRONGFUL EVICTION. THE DEMURRER TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF NO LONGER RESIDES IN THE UNIT; THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT THIS ACTION IS BROUGHT FOR THE BENEFIT OF THE PUBLIC AND TAKING PROPERTY LEFT BY A TENANT IS NOT AN UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICE. TEN DAYS TO ANSWER THE COMPLAINT. =(302/PJM/AY)
